name: WeKnora
tagline: Open-source RAG framework that turns documents into a queryable knowledge base
seoTitle: WeKnora — Open-Source RAG & Agent Knowledge Framework
metaDescription: WeKnora is Tencent's open-source, self-hostable RAG framework: document Q&A, a ReAct agent that orchestrates tools, and auto-generated Wiki knowledge bases.
overview: WeKnora is an open-source, LLM-powered knowledge framework from Tencent, built for understanding documents, searching them by meaning, and reasoning over the results. It is self-hostable and modular, so you can swap the language model, vector database, and storage backend to fit your setup.

It is organized around three core jobs. RAG-based Quick Q&A answers everyday questions over your knowledge base. A ReAct agent handles harder, multi-step tasks by orchestrating retrieval, MCP tools, and web search on its own. Wiki Mode has agents turn raw documents into a structured, interlinked Markdown knowledge base with an interactive knowledge graph.

WeKnora can pull content in from sources like Feishu, Notion, and Yuque, handles formats including PDF, Word, images, and Excel, and can serve answers through chat channels such as WeCom, Feishu, Slack, and Telegram. It works with major LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), DeepSeek, Qwen, Gemini, and Ollama.
features: Three knowledge modes: RAG-based Quick Q&A, a ReAct agent that autonomously orchestrates retrieval, MCP tools, and web search, plus a Wiki Mode that auto-generates interlinked Markdown pages with a knowledge graph
Wide model support: OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Anthropic (Claude), DeepSeek, Qwen, Zhipu, Hunyuan, Gemini, MiniMax, NVIDIA, and Ollama, with swappable embeddings and vector databases
Multi-format ingestion across PDF, Word, Txt, Markdown, HTML, images, CSV, Excel, PPT, and JSON, plus auto-sync from Feishu, Notion, and Yuque
Multiple retrieval strategies including BM25 sparse search, dense retrieval, GraphRAG, parent-child chunking, and HNSW-accelerated pgvector
Enterprise access control with tenant RBAC (Owner / Admin / Contributor / Viewer roles), per-knowledge-base ownership, and a per-tenant audit log
Built-in Langfuse observability for tracing ReAct loops, token usage, tool calls, and the document-parsing pipeline

gettingStarted.intro: WeKnora runs through Docker Compose. You need Docker, Docker Compose, and Git installed, then clone the repo, set up the environment file, and bring up the services.

steps.heading: Clone and start the services
steps.body: Clone the repository, copy the example environment file (edit it as needed), then start the core services with Docker Compose.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/Tencent/WeKnora.git
cd WeKnora
cp .env.example .env   # Edit .env as needed
docker compose up -d   # Start core services

steps.heading: Open the Web UI
steps.body: Once the services are running, visit http://localhost to start. The backend API is available at http://localhost:8080.

steps.heading: Enable optional services
steps.body: Add Docker Compose profile flags to turn on extra components such as the Neo4j knowledge graph, MinIO object storage, or Langfuse tracing. Profiles can be combined.
steps.code:
docker compose --profile full up -d              # All features
docker compose --profile neo4j --profile minio up -d

steps.heading: Drive it from the CLI
steps.body: The official weknora CLI lets you log in, manage knowledge bases, upload documents, and chat straight from the terminal.
steps.code:
weknora auth login --host https://kb.example.com
weknora kb list
weknora doc upload notes.md
weknora chat "summarise the design doc"

useCases: Build a private, self-hosted question-answering system over internal PDFs, Word docs, and spreadsheets, keeping all data on your own infrastructure
Auto-generate a maintained internal wiki from scattered documents, complete with interlinked pages and a knowledge graph
Deploy a ReAct agent that combines document retrieval, MCP tools, and web search to handle complex multi-step research questions
Serve knowledge-base answers directly inside chat channels like WeCom, Feishu, Slack, or Telegram for team self-service
